description: Handle workspace and project deletion cleanup for Firebase Storage images, and ensure edge cases (loading states, error handling) are robust
steps: Step 1 - Find the workspace deletion logic in App.jsx (search for deleteWorkspace or removeWorkspace functions). When a workspace is deleted, gather all image IDs from that workspace's images array and call deleteWorkspaceImages(activeProjectId, workspaceId, imageIds) to remove files from Storage.

Step 2 - Find the project deletion logic (deleteProject function). When a project is deleted, iterate through all workspace data for that project and delete all Storage images. Call deleteWorkspaceImages for each workspace.

Step 3 - Add error resilience: If uploadImage fails (network error, Storage not configured), the image should still be usable with a fallback. Consider storing a local object URL as temporary src so the user sees the image immediately, then replace with Storage URL when upload completes. If upload fails completely, show a toast/warning but keep the local preview.

Step 4 - Handle the case where isFirebaseConfigured() returns false: All image operations should fall back gracefully. uploadImage should return null, and images without URLs should still display if they have a local blob URL or the legacy src field.
acceptance_criteria: Deleting a workspace also removes its images from Firebase Storage
Deleting a project removes all its images from Firebase Storage
Image upload gracefully handles errors without breaking the UI
When Firebase is not configured, images still work locally (degraded mode)
verification: Run `npm run build` in /projects/sandbox/ClearMind and confirm it succeeds
Grep App.jsx for deleteWorkspace and verify Storage cleanup is called
Verify imageStorageService functions handle errors gracefully (try/catch with console.warn)
findings: Most of the acceptance criteria (workspace deletion cleanup, project deletion cleanup, isFirebaseConfigured guards, try/catch error handling) were already implemented as part of FEAT-001. The only remaining fix was moving URL.revokeObjectURL inside the success branch of handleCanvasImageDrop so failed uploads preserve the local blob URL preview.
